page: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-400s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. The configuration includes 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation and a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 400 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this system.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics for this hardware configuration. The AMD EPYC Genoa processor delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% in stress-ng testing. However, multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average tier. Memory subsystem performance is strong, with write bandwidth for large blocks and PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25%, while read-only bandwidth remains average. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show strong results, though multi-threaded decompression is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

The instance exhibits notable tradeoffs across different workloads. While single-core execution is highly efficient, database operations and static web serving throughput are relatively weak, scoring in the bottom 25%. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference with a small 135M model achieves average speeds, but prompt processing for a medium 7B model drops to the bottom 10%. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 paired with local NVMe storage offers a dense resource profile for memory-bound tasks without the cost of dedicated CPU cores. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, and moderate-scale data processing workloads that benefit from high single-core performance and fast local storage.
description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-400s-amd is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a bundled 400 GB local NVMe SSD. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth, though multi-core scaling and database operations are weaker. The instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads by pairing a dense RAM allocation with local NVMe storage on shared CPU resources. It is suited for memory-bound applications, caching systems, and single-threaded processing tasks.
